🔥 43 days since their last loss 
🔥 Third team to win Game 1 & 2 of NBA Finals on the road

🔥 8th straight road playoff win

items.title: Five teams have come back from a 0-2 deficit in the Finals.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
Five teams have come back from a 0-2 deficit in the Finals.

▪️ Boston 1969
▪️ Portland 1977
▪️ Miami 2006
▪️ Cavaliers 2016
▪️ Milwaukee 2021

All went down on the road. None at home.

Can the Spurs defy history?

#NBA

items.title: Wrote about Game 2, Wemby, and the difficulty of quantifying a lost opportunity.
items.author: Mike Finger
items.body_excerpt:
Wrote about Game 2, Wemby, and the difficulty of quantifying a lost opportunity. 

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

images.alt:
After he passed the ball to a teammate who wasn't looking, and after he missed the shot he could have made in his sleep, Victor Wembanyama finished his night the way he always does.

He walked to the back corner of the Spurs locker room, by the entrance to the showers, and stepped on a high-tech scale.

To calculate what he'd lost.

The red digital readout did not account for everything. Pounds, ounces and body-fat percentage can be measured. Opportunity tends to be harder to quantify, especially once it's slipped from your fingers and off the back of the rim.

To be sure, Wembanyama will have lots of others, and so will the Spurs, even after Friday's 105-104 loss to the New York Knicks in Game 2 of the NBA Finals at Frost Bank Center. But it might be a while before they fully realize how much they could have gained with one more basket, or how often their older selves will wonder what might have changed if they had one play to do over again.

"Yeah, I threw that one away," Wembanyama said. "I messed up."

items.title: Stephon Castle’s struggles in Game 2 stemmed from a problem that's plagued him throughout the playoffs.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Stephon Castle’s struggles in Game 2 stemmed from a problem that's plagued him throughout the playoffs.

"He probably just got caught up in the air a few times trying to make decisions," Mitch Johnson said.

Castle agreed, calling a “fair” assessment.

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

items.title: Shortly after the Knicks survived a furious Spurs comeback, I asked Walt "Clyde" Frazier if he thought we'd be coming back to San Antonio.
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt:
Shortly after the Knicks survived a furious Spurs comeback, I asked Walt "Clyde" Frazier if he thought we'd be coming back to San Antonio.

"I don't think so," he said. "I think it's our destiny now, the way things have unfolded for the Knicks."

At Y! Sports: On Game 2 and destiny: yhoo.it/4xuGqA7
